text:
Team GB star Josh Kerr believes he was ‘disrespected’ by a fellow Olympian as he explained his nasty feud with fierce 1500m rival Jakob Ingebrigtsen.

Kerr clinched a silver medal for Great Britain in the 1500m final at theOlympic GamesinParison Tuesday,missing out on goldto Team USA’s Cole Hocker.

Norwegian runner Ingebrigtsen failed to make the podium – finishing fourth behind American Yared Nuguse – who walked away with the bronze medal.

After the final, Kerr, who ran the race in a British-record time of 3:27.79, was asked about Ingebrigtsen andexplained how the rivalry intensifiedin Budapest last year.

‘Over the years there has been a bit of back and forth,’ Kerr toldEurosportwhen asked to ‘spill the tea’ about his bitter feud with Ingebrigtsen.

‘I felt a little bit disrespected last year after my first world title (at the 2023 World Championships in Budapest, Hungary).

‘There is something about someone saying they are not 100 per cent when you go out and get your first world title that makes it sting a bit.

‘I want everyone to be at their best and I want to beat everyone at their best. That’s where it started for me, and I just started being honest with the questions.

‘I was asked and instead of giving everyone the benefit of the doubt, I was being myself without much filter.

‘I think that annoyed some people and turned some heads. Running and athletics is a gentleman’s sport so there’s not a lot of that, but it is what it is.’

Josh Kerr spills the tea on his rivalry with Jakob Ingebrigtsen âð#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/3Bq6C5JBn5

Ingebrigtsen, who said he could beat Kerr blindfolded earlier this year, posted about the Briton on Instagram after Tuesday night’s race at the Stade de France.

‘Well, I guess he did show up after all…!’ Ingebrigtsen said about Kerr on his social media account.

‘Hocker and Nuguse as well… My team always say that ‘because you have a big mouth and is the one to beat, you have everything to lose in competitions’.

Jakob Ingebrigtsen aimed a massive dig at Josh Kerr in February.

‘It is not certain that [Kerr] runs any better now than last year at the same time; he did not run two miles then,’ Ingebrigtsen toldTV2in Norway.

‘I would have beaten him in that race, blindfolded… But it’s good that people run better than they have done before.’

‘Today, Cole Hocker, Yared Nuguse and Josh Kerr outsmarted me.

‘They were ‘the best guys’ when it really mattered. And I want to congratulate them all on a great performance! Of course, I am disappointed!

‘Thank you also to all my competitors for putting on a great show today. Congratulations to the guys bringing home the medals!’

title: Team GB star 'disrespected' by fellow Olympian as he explains nasty feud | Metro News
description: Team GB star Josh Kerr says he was 'disrespected' by a fellow Olympian as he explains his nasty feud with fierce rival Jakob Ingebrigtsen.
